rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL ELY ET AL VS. UNITED SERVICES AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, April 3, 2025 Line 7. PLAINTIFFs MICHAEL ELY,and ALLYSON ELY's MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS. Plaintiffs Michael Ely and Allyson Ely's motion to quash subpoena to David Smith is GRANTED. Any party seeking to subpoena the personal records of a consumer "shall serve or cause to be served on the consumer whose records are being sought . . . notice described in subdivision (e) . . . ." (C.C.P. 1985.3(b).) Here, Defendant United Services Automobile Association never served the separate notice to consumer in compliance with C.C.P. 1985.3(e). Defendant contends that it properly served the subpoena on non-attorney Smith, he was freelancing as a public insurance adjustor, and he individually maintained the records. There is no evidence to support this assertion. The subpoena itself references Smith as a consultant for the law firm. (Oksenendler Decl., Ex. 2.) It even asked for the "entire file" and any and all attorney communications. The only reasonable inference is that Defendant was seeking documents from an attorney and failed to serve the required consumer notice. (C.C. P. 1985.3(a)(1).) In fact, Defendant proffers no opposing evidence and the Gerking reply declaration clarifies that Mr. Smith was working for the law firm and the firm alone maintained the records pertaining to plaintiffs. The subpoena and failure to provide consumer notice lacked substantial justification. Defendant and/or its counsel shall remit $500 in sanctions to plaintiff no later than 10 court days from notice of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD NORMAN VS. PATRICK STRATEMAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, April 3, 2025 Line 13. DEFENDANTs PATRICK STRATEMAN, INTERSANGO LLC,and INTERSANGO, LTD Amended DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT. Defendants Patrick Strateman, Intersango LLC and Intersango, Ltd's Demurrer to Plaintiff Donald Norman's First Amended Complaint is OFF CALENDAR, A party demurring to a pleading must meet and confer with the party who filed the pleading "[b]efore filing [the] demurrer." (CCP section 430.41 (a).) The meeting must be "in person, by telephone or by video conference." (Ibid.) The meeting must take place at least five days before the date the motion must be filed. (CCP section 430.41(a)(2.) The moving party must file a declaration with its moving papers demonstrating compliance with these requirements and/or that the opposing party failed to confer in good faith. (CCP section 430.41(3).) Defendants here have shown they emailed Plaintiff's counsel, but they did not establish that they met-or made any effort to meet-in person, by telephone or by video conference as to the First Amended Complaint at any relevant time. Defendants established they met by video conference on November 4, 2024, but that was clearly to discuss a demurrer to the initial complaint, which was superseded when Plaintiff filed the FAC on December 16, 2024. Defendants did not establish they ever met and conferred regarding the FAC in person, by telephone or by video conference. And, of course, this demurrer is addressed not the original complaint, but the FAC. Due to Defendant's failure to satisfy the statutory requirements, the motion is ordered off calendar. Defendants are granted 20 days leave to respond to the FAC. Plaintiff shall forthwith lodge a proposed order consistent with this tentative. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
